gloss.en: natural substance in food needed for health

words.example: Oranges are full of vitamin C.

gloss.en: a singer, especially in a band

words.example: She is the lead vocalist in the band.

gloss.en: the amount of space something fills

words.example: How do you measure the volume of a gas?

gloss.en: done willingly, not by force

words.example: They signed a voluntary agreement.

gloss.en: a person who works without being paid

words.example: Schools need volunteers to help children read.

gloss.en: food brought up from the stomach

words.example: The floor was covered in vomit.

gloss.en: a person who votes in an election

words.example: Every voter has the right to choose.

gloss.en: the act of choosing in an election

words.example: He was eliminated in the first round of voting.

gloss.en: money you earn each week for work

words.example: He earns a wage of £300 a week.

gloss.en: the narrow part in the middle of the body

words.example: The dress is tight around the waist.

gloss.en: a sleeveless jacket worn over a shirt

words.example: He wore a suit with a matching waistcoat.

gloss.en: a period of time spent waiting

words.example: We had a long wait for the bus.

gloss.en: a casual walk among crowds by an official

words.example: The mayor went on a walkabout in the town.

gloss.en: a person who walks for exercise or pleasure

words.example: The path is popular with walkers.

gloss.en: a board used to cover inside walls

words.example: They covered the wall with wallboard.

gloss.en: a hard nut with a wrinkled shell

words.example: She added a walnut to the cake.

gloss.en: to walk slowly with no clear purpose

words.example: She wandered aimlessly around the streets.

gloss.en: a lack or need of something

words.example: The plants died for want of water.

gloss.en: the process of becoming warm or warmer

words.example: Scientists are worried about global warming.

gloss.en: a ship built for use in war

words.example: The warship sailed into the harbour.

gloss.en: a period when a country is at war

words.example: Food was scarce in wartime.

gloss.en: a flying insect with a sting

words.example: A wasp landed on my arm.

gloss.en: not letting water pass through

words.example: I need a waterproof jacket for the rain.

gloss.en: by a large amount; very far

words.example: She finished the race way ahead of the others.

gloss.en: in a weak way; without strength

words.example: He smiled weakly and lay back down.

gloss.en: lack of physical strength

words.example: The disease causes muscle weakness.

gloss.en: a large amount of money or property

words.example: He used his wealth to help others.

gloss.en: having a lot of money or possessions

words.example: She comes from a wealthy family.

gloss.en: making you feel tired or bored

words.example: The long journey was wearisome.

gloss.en: very tired, especially after hard work

words.example: She felt weary after the long walk.

gloss.en: to marry someone

words.example: They plan to wed next spring.

gloss.en: happening once a week

words.example: We have weekly meetings on Monday.

gloss.en: very strange and hard to explain

words.example: I had a really weird dream last night.

gloss.en: the health, happiness and safety of someone

words.example: We are concerned about the child's welfare.

gloss.en: having a good and healthy mix of things

words.example: A well-balanced diet keeps you healthy.

gloss.en: having a strong, solid body

words.example: He is tall and well-built.

gloss.en: planned and arranged in a good way

words.example: The event was well-organised and ran smoothly.

gloss.en: planned and arranged in a good way

words.example: She is a well-organized worker.

gloss.en: earning a lot of money

words.example: He has a well-paid job in the city.

gloss.en: in or facing the west

words.example: They live in western Spain.

gloss.en: a grain used to make flour for bread

words.example: The bread is made from wheat flour.

gloss.en: used to contrast two facts

words.example: Some studies show good results, whereas others do not.

gloss.en: in or to any place

words.example: Sit wherever you like.

gloss.en: a strong alcoholic drink made from grain
gloss.zh: 威士忌（爱尔兰/美式拼写）

words.example: He poured a glass of whiskey.

gloss.en: a strong alcoholic drink made from grain

words.example: Scotland is famous for its whisky.

gloss.en: a very quiet, soft voice

words.example: He spoke in a whisper.

gloss.en: any person who; the person who

words.example: Whoever says that is a liar.

gloss.en: the object form of 'who'

words.example: Whom did they invite?

gloss.en: by many people; in many places

words.example: The term is widely used in everyday speech.

gloss.en: happening over a large area or to many

words.example: The storm caused widespread damage.

gloss.en: a wild area where no people live

words.example: They hiked deep into the wilderness.

gloss.en: wild animals and plants in nature

words.example: These policies protect wildlife.

gloss.en: ready and happy to do something

words.example: Many people are willing to pay more for organic food.

gloss.en: in a happy and ready way

words.example: She willingly helped with the work.

gloss.en: a victory in a game or contest

words.example: The team had two wins this month.

gloss.en: to follow a path with many bends

words.example: The path wound down to the beach.

gloss.en: having many bends and turns

words.example: We drove along a winding road.

gloss.en: the front window of a car

words.example: Rain hit the windshield hard.

gloss.en: a quick closing of one eye as a signal

words.example: He gave me a friendly wink.

gloss.en: thin metal thread

words.example: She used a coil of copper wire.

gloss.en: good sense gained from experience

words.example: She was a woman of great wisdom.

gloss.en: able to make good, sensible decisions

words.example: He is a wise old man.

gloss.en: to take money out of a bank account

words.example: You can withdraw up to £300 a day.

gloss.en: a person who sees an event happen

words.example: He failed to interview a key witness.

gloss.en: clever and amusing in speech

words.example: Her witty remarks made us laugh.

gloss.en: a wild animal like a large dog

words.example: A wolf howled in the night.

gloss.en: strange and wonderful

words.example: We saw a wondrous sight in the sky.

gloss.en: the art of cutting shapes from wood

words.example: He sells his woodcarving at the market.

gloss.en: without using any words

words.example: She wordlessly handed me the letter.

gloss.en: a person who works too much

words.example: My boss is a real workaholic.

gloss.en: a strong table for doing work

words.example: His tools lay on the workbench.

gloss.en: all the people who work for a company

words.example: The factory has a large workforce.

gloss.en: a man who works with his hands

words.example: A workman came to fix the roof.

gloss.en: the place where people work

words.example: New technology was introduced into the workplace.

gloss.en: a class where people learn by doing

words.example: We learned a lot in the drama workshop.

gloss.en: a table used for doing work

words.example: She spread the cloth on the worktable.

gloss.en: having much experience of life

words.example: He is a worldly and confident man.

gloss.en: the way a person sees the world

words.example: Travel changed her whole worldview.

gloss.en: affecting all parts of the world

words.example: There was an increase in worldwide sales.

gloss.en: a long thin soft creature with no legs

words.example: The birds were looking for worms.

gloss.en: too old or damaged to use; very tired

words.example: His shoes were old and worn-out.

gloss.en: too old or damaged to use; very tired

words.example: She felt completely wornout after work.

gloss.en: making you feel anxious

words.example: The test results are worrying.

gloss.en: of poorer quality; more unpleasant

words.example: The rooms were awful and the food was worse.

gloss.en: to become or make worse

words.example: His health began to worsen.

gloss.en: of the lowest quality of all

words.example: It was the worst speech he ever made.

gloss.en: having a particular value in money

words.example: Our house is worth about £100,000.

gloss.en: having no value or use

words.example: The old coins are now worthless.

gloss.en: an injury, often where the skin is cut

words.example: He had a deep wound on his leg.

gloss.en: to cover something in paper or cloth

words.example: He spent the evening wrapping the presents.

gloss.en: a badly damaged ship, car or building

words.example: Divers explored the old wreck.

gloss.en: the joint between the hand and arm

words.example: She's broken her wrist.

gloss.en: not right or correct

words.example: I got all the answers wrong.

gloss.en: in a way that is not correct or fair

words.example: He was wrongly accused of the crime.

gloss.en: a photo of the inside of the body

words.example: The doctor took an x-ray of my arm.

gloss.en: a large boat for sailing or racing

words.example: They sailed around the bay on a yacht.

gloss.en: an open-mouth breath when tired or bored

words.example: She covered her mouth to hide a yawn.

gloss.en: to want something very much

words.example: They yearn to go back home.

gloss.en: up until now; so far

words.example: I haven't received a letter from him yet.

gloss.en: young or seeming young

words.example: She has a youthful face and energy.

gloss.en: tasting very good

words.example: This cake is really yummy.

gloss.en: an African animal like a striped horse

words.example: A zebra has black and white stripes.

gloss.en: a fastener with two rows of teeth

words.example: The zip on my jacket is broken.

gloss.en: an area with a particular use or feature

words.example: They live near a war zone.

gloss.en: to move very quickly

words.example: The cars zoom past on the highway.
